description_long: This is a stone water well with a roughly octagonal base and a circular opening, constructed from irregular, dark stone blocks that give it a rugged, hand-built appearance. The well is topped with a simple, horizontal metal bar supported by two vertical posts, one of which features a crank handle, likely for operating a rope or chain mechanism. The interior of the well is hollow, suggesting it is designed to hold or access water. The stonework is uneven and textured, with visible chisel marks and a matte, weathered finish. The metal components are dark and slightly corroded, indicating exposure to the elements. There are no visible decorative elements or coverings, and the structure appears utilitarian and robust.
description: A rugged stone water well with a circular opening, octagonal base, and a simple metal crank bar above. The well is utilitarian, with rough stonework and no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's circular opening, octagonal stone base, and the full length of the metal crank bar with handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone wall and a direct look at the vertical support post of the crank mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the well, the metal bar extending across, and the stonework's rugged texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the vertical post and the depth of the well's opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A horizontal metal bar with a crank handle is present, but no pulley or bucket is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a thick, raised stone wall forming an octagonal barrier.
